Strong Progress Toward FY26 Target
• Government likely to meet FY26 Gross Tax Revenue (GTR) target of ₹40.77 lakh Cr

Minimal Growth Needed to Hit Target

Corporate Tax Leads Growth

GST & Excise Collections

Investor Takeaway
• Strong tax collections signal healthy economic activity
• Provides fiscal stability and policy flexibility for the government

INDIAN IT AI REVENUE BOOM  

Industry Projections  
India's IT sector revenues to hit $315 billion in FY26 (+6%), with AI contributing $10-12 billion (~3-4% share).  

Company Disclosures  
- Infosys: $280M AI revenue (Q3 FY26, 5.5% of total).

reports.text:
RBI PROJECT FINANCE NORMS IMPACT  

Key Restrictions (Effective Oct 1, 2025)  
- Debt:Equity ratio fixed at sanction — no top-up loans allowed during project life.  
- Lenders cannot fund cost overruns/delays beyond approved amount.  
- Aims for developer "skin in the game" and financial discipline.  

Real Estate Implications  
- Banks, NBFCs, HFCs face reduced lending flexibility for projects.  
- Higher risks for lenders on under-construction realty exposures.  

Market Adjustments  
- Discretionary funding slows; enhanced transparency/monitoring expected.  
- Workaround: GCP loans with strict RBI end-use compliance.  

Key Takeaway:  
RBI's rigid project finance rules curb mid-project lending, pressuring real estate developers and lender balance sheets — favour completed projects over under-construction plays.

**METALS & CEMENT | HSBC VIEW**  

**Middle East Supply Impact**  
- **Cement:** Cost inflation from **higher pet coke prices** due to coal price surge.  
- **Coal:** No direct impact on Indian supplies.  
- **Aluminum:** No impact on India; **most vulnerable** if disruptions persist — alumina prices may fall.  
- **Steel:** Some disruption expected; **India unaffected** directly.  

**European Ripple Effects**  
- **European steel costs** to rise from elevated gas prices.  

**Cement Pricing Need**  
- Prices require **4-5% hike** in **April-May** to offset energy cost inflation.  

**Key Takeaway:**  
Cement margins face immediate pet coke pressure requiring April-May price hikes; aluminum carries highest disruption risk while Indian steel/coal relatively insulated.

Gem Aromatics Limited: Promoter increases stake, signals confidence

• Promoter Mr. Vipul Nathalal Parekh purchased an additional 0.57% stake at ₹206.24 per share (~₹6.18 crore) on March 04, 2026, raising promoter holding to 55.63%.

• The company recently secured export orders worth ~USD 1 million from customers in the USA and Europe.

• Commercial production has commenced at the Dahej facility for products including WS05 cooling agents and Safranal citral derivatives.

• Total manufacturing capacity stands at ~16,000 MTPA across Silvassa, Budaun, and the new Dahej plant, which is designed as a multi purpose specialty chemical facility.

• The company is targeting revenue of ~₹1,100 crore by FY28 with EBITDA margins of around 16–18%, supported by capacity expansion and new product launches.

CEMENT INDUSTRY PARADOX  

Current Reality  
- Capacity utilisation: 60-70% (decent overcapacity).  
- Pricing power: Negative (-1% to -2% CAGR FY23-26).  
- Margins under pressure from weak pricing.  

Counterintuitive Expansion  
- 158 MT new capacity adding FY25-28.  
- UltraTech + Ambuja account for 50%+ of expansion.  

Why Expand Despite Weak Fundamentals?  
- Demand bet: Massive infra (govt capex), data centers, real estate revival.  
- Capacity war: Fail to expand = lose market share to competitors.  

Q3 Profit Reality  
- Profits +46% YoY driven by cost efficiencies (fuel, logistics), not pricing.  

Investor Perspective  
- High-conviction infra growth play, not quality business moat.  
- Risk: Infra slowdown traps firms with excess capacity, zero pricing power.  

Key Takeaway:  
Cement expansion is a bold infrastructure demand wager amid overcapacity — rewards infra bulls, punishes if capex falters.

- Higher crude to impact consumer stocks via **three channels**:

Infosys–Intel Tie-Up to Accelerate Enterprise AI Adoption

Infosys and Intel announced a strategic partnership to help enterprises scale AI from pilot projects to full production deployments.

The collaboration will combine Intel’s high-performance computing platforms with Infosys Topaz and AI services.

Solutions will be optimized for Intel Xeon processors, Intel Gaudi AI accelerators, and Intel AI PCs.

The partnership aims to create open standards across the edge-to-cloud AI ecosystem.

Focus areas include model management, data integration, performance monitoring, and built-in security features.

The companies will jointly design, develop, and benchmark enterprise AI workloads tailored to business needs.

AI solutions will support IT operations, developer productivity, and automation-led workflows.

The platform will also enable AI agents that can access enterprise data and coordinate tasks within governance frameworks.

SAR TELEVENTURE LIMITED: Announces Acquisition of Grand Foundry Limited

• 🤝 Acquisition Update: SAR Televenture has entered into a Share Purchase Agreement to acquire 70.17% stake in Grand Foundry Limited from existing promoters at ₹1.50 per share. 

• 📊 Open Offer Plan: The company will also acquire up to 26% additional stake from public shareholders at ₹2.50 per share through an open offer as per SEBI regulations. 

• 🏭 Strategic Diversification: Grand Foundry operates in iron, steel and telecom related segments, enabling SAR Televenture to diversify and consolidate its business portfolio. 

• 📈 Control Acquisition: Post completion and full open offer acceptance, SAR Televenture could hold up to 96.17% ownership, gaining effective control of the target company. 

• ⏳ Timeline: The acquisition process is expected to complete within six months of the public announcement. 

🟢 Impact: Positive
Strategic acquisition provides business diversification and potential restructuring opportunity, strengthening SAR Televenture’s long term growth strategy.

• 📍 Strategic Location: The property is located at Ayodhya Dham Marg, Ranopalli, close to key religious and transport hubs including Ram Janmbhoomi, Hanuman Garhi Temple and Ayodhya Dham Railway Station.

• 🛏️ Property Details: The hotel features 48 rooms across Superior, Premium and Family Premium categories, along with banquet facilities capable of hosting ~100 guests.

reports.text:
Goldman Sachs: Oil May Surge to $100

Supply Risk:  
- Strait of Hormuz disruptions could drive oil to $100/bbl if exports stay at ~15% normal for days.  
- Worst-case: 5 more weeks pushes Brent above $100.  

Base Forecast:  
- Brent averages ~$76/bbl in Q2 if supply stabilizes.  

Historical Note:  
- Last above $100 in 2022 post Russia-Ukraine war.  

Market Impact:  
- Fuels inflation, widens trade deficits, pressures equities – hits oil importers like India hard.  

Key Takeaway:  
Prolonged Hormuz risks threaten renewed oil spike and global market volatility.

• 🏛️ Tax Order Issued: Polycab India Limited received an assessment order from the Income Tax Authority for AY 2024-25 (FY 2023-24).

• 📊 Financial Impact: Management stated the order is not expected to materially impact financials or operations.

🟡 Impact: Neutral to Slight Negative
Tax demand may create near-term sentiment pressure, but company expects no material financial impact and is challenging the order legally.

reports.text:
Goldman Sachs Warns Oil Could Surge to $100

• ⚠️ Supply Risk: Goldman Sachs warns oil prices could jump to $100/barrel if disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz persist.

• 📉 Export Disruption: Scenario assumes exports remain at ~15% of normal levels for several days before gradually recovering.

• 📊 Base Case Forecast: Brent crude expected to average ~$76/barrel in Q2 if supply stabilises.

• 🔥 Worst-Case Scenario: Extended disruption for 5 more weeks could push Brent above $100.

• 📅 Historical Context: Brent last crossed $100 in 2022 after the Russia–Ukraine war.

🔴 Impact: Negative for Global Markets
Higher oil prices can raise inflation, widen trade deficits, and pressure equity markets, especially for oil-importing countries like India.

DLF Limited ; Co Clarifies Media Report on Gurgaon Housing Project

• 📰 Media Report Denied: DLF stated that the news headline “No Water, No Power at DLF’s Luxury Homes in Gurgaon” is misleading and sensationalised.

• 🏗️ Project Details: The ‘The Primus’ project in New Gurgaon was developed by its subsidiary DLF Home Developers Ltd, with possession given in 2017 with required utilities.

• ⚖️ Legal Matter: Issue arises from complaints by 5 allottees, earlier decided by NCDRC in May 2023.

• 🏛️ Supreme Court Status: Both parties have appealed in the Supreme Court, which has ordered an inquiry and stated no final opinion has been expressed yet.

• 📊 Business Impact: Company said the matter is sub judice and currently has no material impact on operations.

🟡 Impact: Neutral
Clarification reduces misinformation risk; no immediate financial or operational impact indicated.

reports.text:
Uranium Set for Commodity Supercycle

Supply Lockup:  
- Kazatomprom (20% global production) growth pre-allocated via state deals: India (50% book value), China/Bannerman, Russia/Kazakhstan.  
- Incremental supply bypasses open market; West sidelined.  

Demand Surge:  
- India targets 10x nuclear growth to 100GW (~50 Mlb/yr uranium; 60 Mlb upfront at 50% achievement).  
- New reactors buy 3 yrs supply upfront amid shrinking secondary stocks.  

Market Dynamics:  
- Structural deficit persists; mine timelines span decades.  

Key Takeaway:  
Pre-sold supply + exploding demand mathematically ensures sustained higher uranium prices.

📍 ORDER WIN

MRF: Signs ₹5,300 crore MoU with Tamil Nadu for new tyre manufacturing plant at SIPCOT Industrial Park.

APOLLO MICRO SYSTEMS: Secures ₹73.33 crore defence and government orders including missile and avionics systems.

THE GREAT EASTERN SHIPPING COMPANY: To acquire secondhand Kamsarmax dry bulk carrier via internal accruals.

📍 COLLABORATION

PARAS DEFENCE: Signs MoU with South Korea’s Green Optics for defence and space optics systems.

SALZER ELECTRONICS: Partners with Finland-based Wirepas to expand smart metering solutions.

📍 LEADERSHIP CHANGES

CYIENT: Appoints Prabhakar Atla as COO and Shrinivas Kulkarni as CFO effective April 1, 2026.

SAVITA OIL TECHNOLOGIES: Executive Director Vishal Sood passes away.

📍 NEW PROJECTS

JYOTI STRUCTURES: Commissions 400 kV Gadag-II transmission line project in Karnataka.

MAHINDRA HOLIDAYS & RESORTS INDIA: Signs new 110-key beachside resort in Dapoli, Maharashtra.

SUBA HOTELS: Signs term sheet for 40-room Click Hotels property in Vijayawada.

MODIS NAVNIRMAN: Secures Mumbai redevelopment project with ₹160 crore GDV.

📍 FUNDRAISING

STEEL EXCHANGE INDIA: Plans ₹350 crore preferential warrant issue at ₹9.45 per warrant.

BHARAT FORGE: Approves ₹800 crore unsecured term loan within existing borrowing limits.

INDIAN RAILWAY FINANCE CORPORATION: Conducts ECB roadshow in Singapore to diversify global funding sources.

📍 USFDA / REGULATORY

GLENMARK PHARMA: Receives USFDA approval with 180-day exclusivity for Fluticasone inhaler in US.

GLAND PHARMA: Gets USFDA tentative approval for Brimonidine Tartrate ophthalmic solution.

AUROBINDO PHARMA: Subsidiary launches Pomalidomide capsules in US oncology market.

SMRUTHI ORGANICS: Receives EU GMP inspection attestation for Solapur API facility.

CENTRAL BANK OF INDIA: Gets CCI approval to raise stake to 26% in insurance JVs.

360 ONE WAM: SEBI updates AIF reporting framework with new quarterly and annual mandates.

📍 CLARIFICATION

FINO PAYMENTS BANK: Clarifies on SFB conversion timeline and denies shell company links amid DGGI probe.

MAHINDRA & MAHINDRA: Refutes Indonesia vehicle import suspension reports, confirms advance received.

PIRAMAL FINANCE: Denies media report on MF distribution deal talks with IIFL Finance.

📍 LAUNCHES

CARTRADE TECH: Launches AI-powered transaction tool “SuperDost” across marketplace platforms.

📍 INCORPORATE / CORPORATE STRUCTURE

CYIENT: NCLT approves liquidation of 50:50 JV Infotech HAL Ltd.

HINDUSTAN UNILEVER: Completes sale of 19.8% stake in Nutritionalab for ~₹307 crore.

📍 ADDITIONS

ANONDITA MEDICARE: Gets certification for enhanced production capacity, raising total capacity to ~1,417 million pieces annually.

📍 OTHERS

ADDICTIVE LEARNING: LawSikho learners clear Supreme Court AOR exam, highlighting training outcomes.

SUGAR STOCKS: Rally on rising global sugar prices and improved ethanol economics.

INTERGLOBE AVIATION: Cancels 500+ flights amid Middle East airspace restrictions.

INTERGLOBE AVIATION: Resumes key international and UAE repatriation flights.

GUJARAT GAS: Declares force majeure amid RLNG supply disruption due to Middle East crisis.

ESCORTS, MAHINDRA & MAHINDRA, VST TILLERS, ACE: Impacted as India proposes stricter emission norms for tractors and construction equipment.

reports.text:
360 ONE WAM ;  Securities and Exchange Board of India Updates Reporting Rules for AIFs

• 📑 New Reporting Framework: SEBI has revised regulatory reporting requirements for Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs).

• 🗓️ Quarterly Reporting: AIFs must submit a Limited Quarterly Activity Report within 15 days of each quarter end via the SEBI Intermediary Portal.

• 📊 Annual Report Mandate: A comprehensive Annual Activity Report must be submitted within 30 days after the financial year ends.

• ⚙️ Ease of Doing Business: Revised formats aim to simplify compliance and reduce reporting costs for AIFs.

• 📌 Immediate Effect: The circular comes into force with immediate effect and replaces earlier reporting provisions.

🟡 Impact: Neutral
Primarily a regulatory compliance update for AIFs; limited direct impact on listed equities but improves transparency in the alternative investment ecosystem.

reports.text:
Supreme Power Equipment Limited Begins Operations at New Facility; Capacity Expands to ~9,000 MVA

Supreme Power Equipment Limited has commenced commercial production at its new high-capacity transformer manufacturing facility located at Kannur Village, Thiruvallur District, near Chennai, Tamil Nadu

Key highlights of the new facility:
* Designed to manufacture power transformers up to 200 MVA and 220 kV
* Annual production capacity of 6,000–6,500 MVA
* Total installed manufacturing capacity now ~9,000 MVA per annum

Earlier, the company operated with an installed capacity of 2,500 MVA for transformers up to 110 kV, with capacity utilisation ranging between 70%–80%. The new facility significantly enhances manufacturing bandwidth by enabling production of higher voltage and larger MVA-rated transformers, allowing participation in more complex and large-scale power infrastructure projects

The expansion involves an investment of approximately ₹100 Cr and above towards infrastructure, advanced manufacturing capabilities and supporting facilities. The project has been funded through a balanced mix of IPO proceeds, internal accruals, debt and proceeds from a preferential issue, ensuring a diversified and prudent financing structure

The capacity augmentation is aligned with rising demand in the power transmission and distribution sector and is expected to enable execution of larger MVA contracts, strengthen medium-term revenue visibility and support sustained growth across utilities, industrial and infrastructure segments

ICE MAKE REFRIGERATION LTD – INVESTOR PRESENTATION KEY HIGHLIGHTS

Company Overview
• Founded in 1993, headquartered in Ahmedabad, Gujarat
• Manufactures industrial and commercial refrigeration solutions
• Strategy theme: “From Core to More”
• Moving from equipment supplier to complete cold chain solutions provider

Business Segments & Revenue Mix
• Cold Rooms – 51% (largest revenue contributor)
• Ammonia Refrigeration & Projects – 17%
• Commercial Refrigeration – 16%
• Transport Refrigeration – 8%
• Continuous PUF Panels – 3%
• Industrial Refrigeration – 3%
• Commercial Freezers – 2%

Industry Exposure
• Dairy & Ice Cream – 30–40%
• Food Processing & Hospitality – 15–20%
• Pharma & Chemicals – 15–20%
• E-commerce – 5–7%
• Logistics – 3–5%

Product Portfolio
• Cold Rooms for agriculture, pharma and food processing
• Commercial Refrigeration for hotels and retail stores
• Industrial Refrigeration Systems for large processing plants
• Transport Refrigeration for temperature-controlled logistics
• Solar Cold Rooms for agriculture storage
• Ammonia Refrigeration Systems for large industrial cooling

Technology & New Product Focus
• Quick commerce refrigeration solutions for dark stores
• Visi coolers for retail beverage display
• Continuous PUF sandwich panels for cold storage construction
• Focus on energy-efficient and modular refrigeration systems

Manufacturing Infrastructure
• Plants located in Dantali, Vamaj and Bavla (Gujarat)
• Expansion presence in Kolkata and Chennai
• Manufacturing network improves logistics efficiency and market access

Global Presence
• Exports to 24+ countries
• Key regions: Middle East, Africa, South Asia and Australia
• Export markets include UAE, Nigeria, Kenya, Nepal, Bangladesh and Japan

Revenue Growth Track Record
• FY21 – ₹135 Cr
• FY22 – ₹206 Cr
• FY23 – ₹313 Cr
• FY24 – ₹379 Cr
• FY25 – ₹480 Cr
• Revenue expanded over 3.5x in four years

Profitability Snapshot
• FY25 EBITDA – ₹43.4 Cr
• EBITDA margin around ~9%
• FY25 PAT – ₹22.9 Cr
• PAT margin about ~4.77%

Balance Sheet Strength
• Net worth around ₹123.7 Cr
• Debt-to-equity – 0.39
• ROE around ~20%
• EPS – ₹14.51

Geographic Revenue Mix
• West India – 51%
• East India – 18%
• South India – 14%
• North India – 11%
• Exports – 3%

Structural Growth Drivers
• Rapid growth in cold chain infrastructure demand
• Expansion of processed food and frozen food consumption
• Rising pharma cold chain logistics
• Growth of quick commerce and organized retail
• Government schemes supporting agri cold storage

Sustainability Initiatives
• Solar power generation of ~230 units per day
• Rainwater harvesting systems
• ISO 14001 environmental certification
• Target of Net Zero emissions by 2050

Management Leadership
• Chandrakant P. Patel – Chairman & MD (34+ years experience)
• Rajendra P. Patel – Joint MD (Production & Manufacturing)
• Vipul I. Patel – Joint MD (Finance & Procurement)

Long-Term Strategy
• Expand into complete cold chain infrastructure solutions
• Increase export contribution
• Invest in automation and digital monitoring systems
• Expand manufacturing capacity
• Target revenue of ₹1,000 Cr by FY28

Key Investment Positives
• Strong industry tailwinds in cold chain infrastructure
• Diversified product portfolio across refrigeration segments
• Strong revenue growth track record
• Expansion into quick commerce and retail refrigeration markets
• Growing export presence

Key Risks
• Pressure on margins due to raw material volatility
• Execution risk from rapid capacity expansion
• Dairy sector concentration risk
• Capital intensive manufacturing business

Investor Perspective
• Ice Make is evolving into an integrated cold chain equipment manufacturer
• Positioned to benefit from growth in food processing, pharma logistics and agriculture storage
• Execution of expansion plans could help it become a major cold chain infrastructure player in India

Data Center and Storage Industry Growth in India

India’s data centre capacity is expected to double in the next few years.

Growth in data centers is being led by hyperscalers, which requires storage for training sets, data logs, model versions and compliances.

Around 80% of hyperscalers storage capacity to reside on HDDs in an AI-centric data center as scale, cost, and density matter.

India's data center industry has been growing at 24% CAGR since 2019.

Industry is expected to add 795 MW of new capacity by 2027 taking the entire capacity to 1825 MW.

India is moving from IT services economy → digital infrastructure economy

Telcos Need ₹35–40 ARPU Boost to Achieve 15% RoCE: Ind-Ra

India Ratings and Research estimates India’s telecom industry needs an additional ₹35–40 increase in ARPU to achieve a 15% return on capital employed (RoCE).

This would require 15–20% ARPU growth from current levels; without tariff hikes, organic ARPU growth is likely to remain around 5%.

The agency maintains a neutral outlook for FY27, noting improving profitability and declining capex intensity are supporting RoCE.

Fixed Wireless Access (FWA) is expected to remain a key driver of non-mobile broadband growth.

Non-mobile broadband subscriber additions have surged, rising over 75% in recent months (as of December 2025).

Ind-Ra expects continued capacity expansion, strong demand, and a supportive regulatory environment over the next 12–18 months, benefiting telecom equipment suppliers and service providers.

reports.text:
⛽️ What $140 Oil Actually Means for India

If crude spikes to $140/barrel, the impact on India goes far beyond fuel prices.

1️⃣ Import Bill Surge
• India’s oil import bill could jump to ~$245 billion
• Massive dollar outflow puts pressure on forex reserves

2️⃣ Trade Deficit Widens
• Every $10 rise in oil → ~0.5% higher trade deficit
• At $140 oil, deficit could move toward 3–4%+

3️⃣ Rupee Pressure
• Higher oil → larger deficit → rupee depreciation
• Weaker rupee makes oil even costlier in INR

4️⃣ Inflation & EMIs Rise
• A $60+ oil spike can lift inflation 2–3%
• RBI may raise interest rates, pushing up loan EMIs

5️⃣ Limited Fiscal Cushion
• Fiscal deficit already around 4.5–5%
• Limited scope for fuel tax cuts or subsidies

6️⃣ Hormuz Risk
• ~50% of India’s crude flows through Strait of Hormuz
• Any disruption could mean price spike + supply shock

7️⃣ Larger Economy, Larger Impact
• Oil share of GDP lower than before
• But import volumes have doubled, increasing absolute risk

8️⃣ Sector Impact
• Airlines, transport, paints, chemicals see margin pressure
• Lower profits → less tax revenue & potential job cuts

9️⃣ Capital Flow Risk
• Oil shock can trigger FII outflows
• Leads to weaker rupee & higher borrowing costs

🔟 Renewables Are Long-Term
• Solar & EVs help structurally
• But cannot offset a sudden oil shock

📌 Bottom Line:
India is stronger today, but oil remains a key macro vulnerability.
A sustained $140 crude shock would hit inflation, currency, growth, and household budgets.

IndiaAI Mission – Low GPU Utilisation Concerns

Allocation Status
• Total GPUs committed: 33,099
• Allocated to workloads: 12,638 GPUs (~38%)
• Actually in use: 7,418 GPUs (~22%)

Idle Capacity
• 5,317 GPUs (~16%) still available for allocation
• Several allocated GPUs remain unused by customers

Reasons for Low Utilisation
• Slow deployment timelines
• Delays in fund approvals and supply chains
• Falling prices of high-end GPUs making government pricing less competitive

Market Dynamics
• Companies exploring cheaper GPU access outside the programme
• Some cloud providers report allocated GPUs not actively used

Policy Adjustment
• Government allows providers to rent unused GPUs outside IndiaAI framework

IndiaAI Initiative
• IndiaAI Compute Portal (2025) created to offer affordable GPU access to startups, researchers and developers

Big Picture
• Infrastructure created, but demand realisation and pricing competitiveness remain key challenges for India’s AI ecosystem.

reports.text:
Singtel to Cut Stake as Airtel Simplifies Promoter Structure

Bharti Airtel plans to consolidate promoter holdings under Bharti Telecom (BTL) as part of a restructuring roadmap.

Bharti Telecom currently holds ~41% stake in Airtel, making it the main promoter vehicle.

About 7% stake held by Singtel and ~1% held by the Mittal family outside BTL will be gradually reduced via market transactions over the next 3–4 years.

After restructuring, Bharti Telecom will remain the sole direct promoter entity with 41% stake in Airtel.

Singtel and the Mittal family will pare their residual direct holdings to zero, increasing the public float.

The plan is part of a broader promoter restructuring first outlined in August 2022 to simplify and equalise holdings.

Overall promoter shareholding in Airtel has already declined from 55.9% (June 2022) to about 48.9% currently.

**BOFA GLOBAL RESEARCH – India Trucking Cycle**

**Industry Outlook**
• India CV industry entering **new upcycle after ~7 years**
• **M&HCV volumes expected ~10% CAGR**
• Demand recovery visible beyond small CV segment

**Key Demand Drivers**
• Return of **small fleet operators** as financing improves
• **Strong freight market** with higher rentals & utilization
• **Replacement cycle overdue** (fleet age **10+ years vs 7–8 earlier**)
• Aging **BS4 trucks reducing used vehicle attractiveness**

**Industry Structure**
• Highly consolidated market structure
• **Tata Motors, Ashok Leyland, VECV** control **~80% share**
• Strong pricing discipline, service network & brand stickiness
• High entry barriers for new players

**Regulatory Tailwinds**
• Focus on **safety, braking systems, ADAS & better cabins**
• Manageable vehicle price inflation
• **Electrification risk low for heavy trucks for ~5 years**

**Profitability Outlook**
• Industry EBITDA margins expected in **mid-teens**
• **Ashok Leyland:** targeting mid-teen margins
• **Tata Motors CV:** teen EBITDA margins expected

**Structural Growth Drivers**
• **Exports growth** in Middle East, Africa & SAARC
• **LCV demand** supported by e-commerce & last-mile logistics
• **Bus demand** rising via replacement programs & **PM e-Bus Sewa**

**Market Structure Insights**

**M&HCV Segment**
• **95% share with three players**
• Tata Motors – Leader
• Ashok Leyland – #2
• VECV – #3

**LCV Segment**
• More fragmented market
• Leaders: **Mahindra & Mahindra, Tata Motors, Ashok Leyland, Maruti Suzuki**

**Industry Economics**
• CV industry **ROE ~25–30%**
• Stable margins vs other auto segments
• Strong profitability visibility

**BofA Investment View – Top Picks**
• **Tata Motors (CV business)**
• **Ashok Leyland**

**Investor Takeaway**
• Industry entering **multi-year trucking upcycle**
• Driven by **replacement demand, freight strength & consolidation**
• Strong earnings visibility for CV OEMs and suppliers 📈

Kotak Inst Eqt on Oil & Gas

Sharp escalation in the conflict involving the US and Israel with Iran, coupled with Iran’s threats to target vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz (SoH), has led to a near standstill in oil flows through this critical global energy chokepoint

The Strait of Hormuz accounts for roughly 20% of global petroleum consumption and LNG trade. India faces elevated exposure, with an estimated 50-55% of its crude oil and LNG imports transiting the SoH.

Given limited strategic reserves (8-9 days for oil and none for gas), India is vulnerable if the disruption persists

Believe that gas supplies will be rationed soon.

For oil refiners, while volumes could be sustained, costs will sharply rise with higher crude/logistics costs.

From Bloomberg:

India holds about 100 million barrels of commercial 
crude oil stocks - in storage tanks, underground strategic 
reserves and on ships voyaging towards the country - which could cover roughly 40-45 days of its requirement if flows through the Strait of Hormuz are disrupted, according to Kpler.
India imports about 88 per cent of the crude oil it needs the raw material for fuels such as petrol and diesel - with more than 50 per cent supplied by Middle Eastern countries and transiting the narrow Strait of Hormuz, flows from which have been disrupted amid the Iran crisis.
If Middle Eastern crude supply were to halt completely for 
a temporary period, the immediate impact would be logistical and price-driven, with supply risks intensifying if movement through the Strait of Hormuz is disrupted for longer, said Sumit Ritolia, Lead Research Analyst, Refining & Modeling at Kpler.
A closure of Strait of Hormuz would at first impact prompt 
cargo liftings. "However, refiners typically maintain commercial inventories, and cargoes already on water would continue to arrive, providing some short-term cushioning to the system," he said adding in the event of a prolonged disruption, medium-term pressures would build through higher import costs, freight exposure and the need to reroute supplies over longer distances.
"The country maintains strategic petroleum reserves 
alongside commercial inventories held by refiners and oil 
marketing companies. These buffers are intended to manage temporary supply shocks rather than sustained outages," he said. 
"Based on Kpler inventory data, commercial crude stocks are 
around 100 million barrels, including volumes in the strategic 
petroleum reserve (SPR) facilities at Mangalore, Padur and 
Visakhapatnam."
With imports via the Strait of Hormuz averaging roughly 
2.5 million barrels per day - about half of India's just over 5 
million bpd total crude imports - these combined reserves could theoretically cover around 40-45 days of imports in a crude disruption scenario, he said.
Additional refined product inventories would extend 
effective coverage further. However, the immediate impact will be on prices. Brent, global benchmark, crossed USD 80 per barrel, roughly 10 per cent more since the Iran crisis. For India, higher prices means higher import bill.
India spent USD 137 billion on crude oil imports in fiscal 
year ended March 31, 2025. During April 2025 to January 2026 - first ten months of current fiscal - it spent USD 100.4 billion on import of 206.3 million tonnes of crude oil.
The United States and Israel launched military strikes on 
targets in Iran over the weekend. Tehran retaliated with 
missiles and drones aimed at Israel and countries hosting US forces, including the United Arab Emirates, Qatar, Kuwait, 
Bahrain, Iraq, Jordan and Saudi Arabia. Media reports suggest the conflict has effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz, a key conduit for global energy flows. Roughly one-third of the world's seaborne crude oil exports and about 20 per cent of liquefied natural gas shipments transit the narrow waterway.
India, the world's third largest oil importer, imports 
roughly half of its crude needs through the narrow Strait. Its 
mainstay liquefied natural gas (LNG) supplier in Qatar also uses the strait to ship the fuel to India. In case of closure, India can tap suppliers in West Africa, Latin Amercia and the US to make up for the shortfall from the Middle-East. India could also tap Russian oil to make up for the deficit.
India had agreed to wind down purchases of Russian oil as 
part of a trade deal with the US - a deal which now sits in 
limbo after the US Supreme Court struck down US President Donald Trump's country-based tariffs. "Russian cargoes currently floating in the Arabian Sea and wider Asian region without firm buyers could also be absorbed relatively quickly if required," Ritolia said.

reports.text:
If the Strait of Hormuz - the narrow 33-kilometre passage 
connecting the Persian Gulf to the Arabian Sea - were disrupted or shipping were forced onto longer routes, India's crude import bill would rise.
Even without a full blockade, higher freight, war-risk 
insurance and geopolitical premiums would lift landed costs.
"In a more severe scenario, policy intervention would 
likely become a key stabilising tool. The government could 
prioritise domestic energy security by asking refiners to 
moderate or temporarily curb refined product exports to ensure adequate domestic supply," he said.
Given that India is among the world's largest exporters of 
refined products, particularly diesel and jet, redirecting 
export volumes to the domestic market would provide an 
additional buffer.
India exported 23.7 million tonnes (474,000 bpd) of 
petroleum products or 10 per cent of the country's fuel 
consumption, in 2024-25. During April-January, exports stood at 53.3 million tones.
"If the Hormuz situation were to deteriorate meaningfully, 
safeguarding domestic fuel availability and price stability 
would likely take precedence over export optimisation, 
reinforcing internal supply resilience even at the cost of lower export revenues," he said.
The worst-case scenario would involve a prolonged and 
severe disruption to Hormuz flows combined with sustained 
geopolitical escalation. In that case, crude prices would spike sharply, freight markets would tighten, and refiners could eventually be forced to trim runs if replacement barrels are delayed.
"However, such a scenario would have major global economic consequences, making it a low-probability but high-impact risk," he said adding the near-term risk is primarily price volatility and higher import costs rather than immediate physical shortage, for now.
India has diversification options and inventory buffers, 
but sustained disruption would materially increase the import bill and create macroeconomic pressures.

FACTORS TO WATCH: MIDDLE EAST WAR DRIVES RISK-OFF

Market Sentiment  
- Risk-off dominates globally; expect gap-down opens amid Asian stocks' 3rd straight fall (Kospi -12% in 2 days).  
- FII shorts at 84% (up from 82%); cash market sellers for 3rd day; all benchmarks below key SMAs/LMAs.  
- Benchmarks trading below key SMAs, LMAs.

Safe Havens  
- Dollar Index above 99, +3rd day (EM negative).  
- Gold +1.4% on haven demand (not bonds).  
- US 10Y Treasury yield +6bps to 4.1%+ on inflation fears delaying Fed cuts; low safe-haven bond bids.

Energy Disruptions  
- Brent crude >$82/bbl on geo-tensions; Qatar/Iraq halt major sites.  
- Trump reassures Hormuz shipping safeguards.

Investor Strategy  
- Long-term: Buy quality dips if unlevered.  
- Short-term: Stay away until settle; celebrate Holi, avoid panic.

Key Takeaway  
Safe-havens (USD, gold) shine amid volatility—watch oil spikes and FII flows for dip-buy entry signals.

JM FINANCIAL ON ADANI ENERGY – BUY | TARGET ₹1,199

• Maintained Buy recommendation; target price at ₹1,199.

• Believes company is strongly positioned to benefit from India’s T&D growth story.

• Growth backed by robust ₹778 billion transmission order book.

• Smart metering portfolio of 24.6 million meters.

• Stable distribution franchise with regulated asset base (RAB) of ₹96 billion.

• Revenue (ex-SCA) / EBITDA / PAT expected to compound at 19% / 15% / 50% over FY25–28E.

Citi on India Strategy

BROKERAGE RADAR

Key Middle East region accounts for 14% of goods exports and 19% of imports, primarily led by the UAE

Share is large in sectors like autos and jewellery for exports and oil and natural gas for imports

Further exposure is via disruption in Strait of Hormuz (50% crude, 60% LNG and almost all LPG imports)

STOCKS WATCHLIST: KEY MOVERS TODAY

Positives  
- Adani Ports: Feb cargo volume hits 42.5 MMT, up 16% YoY on container +20% and dry bulk strength.  
- Tata Motors, M&M: Indonesia 70k-unit export order confirmed on track, deliveries phased smoothly.  
- Natco Pharma: Launches generic Pomalidomide (1-4mg) in USA with $3.2B branded sales backing, 180-day exclusivity.  
- Brigade Enterprises: Secures 90-year lease for 1.2M sq ft IT/office/hotel project at Kerala Technopark.  
- HAL: Bags ₹2,901 Cr MoD contract for 6 ALH MK III helicopters to Coast Guard, signed Mar 3.  
- MAS Financial Services: RBI approves factoring business via new NBFC registration.  
- IOL Chemicals: Expands ethyl acetate to 120k MTPA (+20k), acetic anhydride to 32k MTPA (+7k) at ₹9.71 Cr cost.  
- DCX Systems: Announces fresh capacity addition initiatives.  
- Acme Solar: Inks 450 MW PPA with SJVN (300+150 MW) at ₹6.74/unit, starts Mar 2028 for 25 years.  
- Lemon Tree Hotels: Signs 100-room property deal in UP's Chitrakoot.  
- AGI Infra: QIP opens Mar 4 at floor price ₹274.83/share (up to 5% discount allowed).  

Negatives  
- Greenply Industries: Income Tax Dept launches search & seizure operations.  
- Indian Overseas Bank: Faces ₹766 Cr tax demand notice.  

Key Takeaway  
Strong order wins and capacity expansions dominate positives; tax overhangs warrant caution—focus on defensives amid global volatility.

GLOBAL MARKETS ON EDGE AS US-IRAN CONFLICT ESCALATES

Geopolitical Shockwaves  
- President Trump confirms fresh US strikes on Iran’s new leadership, declaring Tehran “militarily beaten.”  
- Israel bombed Iran’s Assembly of Experts during succession meeting; Israeli forces issued 24-hour ultimatum to Hezbollah.  
- US Consulate in Dubai hit by Iranian drone; UAE intercepts 11 ballistic missiles and 123 drones in one day.  
- Iraq halts output from Rumaila oilfield (3 mbpd risk). Strait of Hormuz tensions disrupting global energy flows.  
- Kuwait reports casualties from Iranian attacks; over 1 million travelers stranded across the region.

Market Reaction  
- Brent crude spiked intraday +9%, eased to +2% after US pledged tanker escorts and risk insurance coverage.  
- Natural gas futures surged to $3.05/MMBtu — highest since mid-February.  
- US indices fell sharply: Dow -415 pts, Nasdaq -233 pts, S&P 500 -67 pts.  
- Fed officials signal pause in rate-cut expectations amid war-driven inflation uncertainty.  
- NVIDIA shutters Dubai office; corporates revising Middle East exposure plans.

Global Alignments Shifting  
- UAE, Kuwait, and Lebanon distancing from Iranian proxies.  
- Trump slams UK and Spain for “non-cooperation,” praises France and Germany.  
- Zelensky offers Ukraine’s air-defense tech to UAE; France deploying warship to Cyprus.  
- Senate to vote on War Powers resolution tomorrow.

Key Takeaway:  
Surging geopolitical risk is reshaping global energy and equity sentiment—investors should expect defensive rotation, higher volatility, and short-term support for oil and defense sectors.

O2C Integration

FY25 Output  
- Total 71.2 MMT: fuels 46.7 MMT (+6.9%), polymers 6 MMT, polyesters 2.8 MMT (+12%), chemicals 12.6 MMT.  

Export + Domestic

- India targets 310 MMTPA by 2028; Jamnagar stays global arbitrage king.

Midcap IT Double Down on AI-Led Mega Deals to Take on Tier-1 Players  

Strategic Pivot to AI-Led Large Deals  
- Mid-tier IT firms are aggressively shifting to AI-first, large multi-year deals to stay relevant amid GenAI disruption and changing client expectations.  
- Narrative is moving from experimentation to AI-native core transformation, with clients demanding clear, outcome-linked value.  

Big Wins for Zensar, LTIMindtree, Coforge  
- Zensar Technologies has bagged a $210 million, 5.5-year deal with a large financial services player, centred on AI-native enterprise transformation and automation.  
- LTIMindtree has secured a $100 million European healthcare contract, along with a large AI-driven tax analytics modernisation deal in India, underscoring its data and AI depth.  
- Coforge has won a $158 million, five-year UK deal and forged a strategic partnership with US-based VHC Health to drive AI-led infrastructure, cloud and digital workplace transformation. 

From Pilots to Full-Scale AI Deployment  
- Clients are moving beyond PoCs and pilots to enterprise-wide AI rollouts, embedding AI into core systems and operations.  
- Boardroom focus is on governance, resilience, operational efficiency and measurable ROI, making AI-linked KPIs central to contract structures.  

Europe Emerges as a Hotspot  
- Deal sizes and win frequency are rising, particularly in Europe, where enterprises are favouring phased modernisation with modular AI capabilities.  
- Midcaps are leveraging speed, flexibility and domain focus to win against larger incumbents in competitive RFPs.  

Competitive Dynamics with Tier-1 IT  
- The shift to AI-embedded, longer-tenure contracts is blurring the line between midcaps and large-cap IT on deal profile.  
- Differentiation is increasingly driven by AI platforms, execution speed and ability to co-innovate rather than just scale and legacy relationships.  

Revenue Visibility and Growth Construct  
- Multi-year, AI-centric deals are becoming the core engine for revenue visibility, supporting better growth predictability for midcap IT.  
- As AI becomes non-discretionary spend, these contracts can lift annuity revenues, expand wallet share and support margin resilience over the cycle.  

Key Takeaway  
AI-led mega deals are turning midcap IT into serious contenders to Tier-1 firms, with long-tenure, outcome-linked contracts poised to structurally rerate growth and visibility for the segment.

Semicon 2.0 to Focus on Chip Design and Deep-Tech Innovation  

Strategic Shift  
- Union IT Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw unveils Semicon Mission 2.0 with focus on design ecosystem and deep-tech startups.  
- Moves beyond manufacturing-led Semicon 1.0 to prioritize chip design, innovation, and equipment manufacturing.  

Core Objective  
- Build a robust semiconductor design ecosystem enabling Indian startups to develop advanced chips and IPs.  
- Government aims to foster companies that could become India’s Qualcomm, Broadcom, or Nvidia.  

Talent & Capability Push  
- India faces a 2 million talent gap in the semiconductor industry—seen as a major opportunity for engineers and students.  
- Initiative to invest in design infrastructure, training, and R&D to close this skills gap.  

Progress So Far  
- 10 semiconductor plants approved under the national mission.  
- One plant already commercial, and another nearing production start.  

Next Steps  
- Align chip design output with domestic fabs for Made-in-India semiconductor production.  
- Scale up equipment manufacturing and design infrastructure to complete the semiconductor value chain.  

Key Takeaway:  
Semicon 2.0 marks India’s pivot from fabs to innovation—aiming to transform the country into a global semiconductor design powerhouse.

Nvidia Teams Up with Telcos to Build AI-Native 6G Networks  

Strategic Alliance  
- Nvidia partners with Nokia, SoftBank Group, and T-Mobile US to co-develop AI-native 6G infrastructure.  
- Target: reinvent how future mobile networks handle AI-driven workloads and intelligent devices.  

Why It Matters  
- Current 5G systems optimized for voice and data—not designed for the coming wave of AI traffic.  
- 6G networks will integrate AI at the core, supporting robots, autonomous systems, and real-time analytics.  

Technology Vision  
- Future architecture to feature AI-powered software, open systems, and programmable radios.  
- Shifts from hardware-heavy designs to cloud-like networks that can self-manage, scale, and adapt rapidly.  

Business Angle  
- Positions Nvidia to expand its AI chips and software ecosystem into telecom infrastructure.  
- Represents a new growth frontier, merging data center intelligence with wireless technology.  

Industry Implication  
- Early collaboration aims to define global 6G standards capable of supporting large-scale AI deployment.  
- Signals the start of convergence between AI computing and next-gen connectivity.  

Key Takeaway:  
Nvidia is moving beyond data centers—embedding AI directly into future telecom networks to power the intelligent, connected economy of the 6G era.

Volvo Car India Drops 2030 All-Electric Goal, Adopts Flexible EV Path  

Strategic Shift  
- Volvo scraps its earlier target to go 100% electric by 2030.  
- New approach: market-driven transition based on infrastructure, policy, and demand.  
- MD Jyoti Malhotra says electrification commitment remains strong but timeline is now flexible.  

Current Performance  
- EVs form 25% of Volvo India sales, well above the segment average of 10–11%.  
- Goal: raise EV share to ~33% of total sales through new launches and ecosystem expansion.  

New Models & Portfolio  
- Two new EVs planned for 2026 — one SUV and one sedan.  
- Company to continue dual strategy: ICE + mild hybrids alongside EVs.  

Sales Trend  
- 2023: 2,120 units | 2024: 1,861 units | 2025: 1,630 units.  
- Early 2026 (till Mar 1): 307 units sold, indicating cautious momentum.  

Ecosystem Investments  
- Focus on ultra-fast charging infra (Mumbai–Nashik corridor pilot).  
- Strengthening after-sales network and parts availability.  

Positioning & Outlook  
- Aims to sustain premium brand status via safety, Scandinavian design, and sustainability.  
- Faces rising EV competition in India’s luxury segment.  

Key Takeaway:  
Volvo is recalibrating its EV roadmap—prioritizing flexibility, infrastructure readiness, and profitability over rigid timelines.

• $10/bbl rise = **$12–13bn** extra annual import bill
• $30/bbl spike = **>70 bps GDP hit**

• $40–50bn non-oil exports via MENA exposed

**Sector View**

• If resolved within **2–3 days**, macro hit limited
• Likely conflict duration **<1 week**
• Prolonged escalation increases downside risk

**Historical Context**
• Most geopolitical shocks fade within a week
• Crude typically spikes **4%+**, extreme case **30%** (Russia-Ukraine)
• This event has more direct macro channels for India

• Crude trajectory is the primary driver

• Fiscal cushioning possible but capex may adjust

Geopolitical shocks rattle markets, but history shows they rarely derail the trend for long.

During major conflict events, the Sensex has typically corrected about 10% and recovered in nearly 38 trading days. After the 2022 Russia invasion, the index fell around 8% but went on to hit fresh highs within 45 days.

Volatility rises on headlines. Markets rebuild on fundamentals. Phases like these often create opportunities to position portfolios for structural alpha.

**Gravita India (CMP: 1,594): Strategic Expansions Fuel Multi-Year Compounder Story**

**Strategic Moves**  
- Acquired Rashtriya Metals Industries (RMIL) for Rs 565 crore – entry into copper recycling/alloys; total copper capacity now 340 ktpa, +45 ktpa commissioning this quarter, fully onstream by Q2FY27.  
- Mundra lead recycling expanded to 145,100 TPA (Rs 49 crore capex via internal accruals).  
- 6,000 TPA Li-ion battery recycling plant commissioned at Mundra (Rs 14 crore capex) – taps EV/clean energy circular economy theme.

**Q3FY26 Performance**  
- Revenue Rs 1,017 crore (+2.1% YoY); EBITDA Rs 111 crore (+25.2% YoY, OPM 11.0% up 200 bps); PAT Rs 98 crore (+25.3% YoY), EPS Rs 13.4.  
- Lead core driver (85-90% volumes/revenue): Q3 lead revenue +9% YoY to Rs 915 crore; EBITDA/kg Rs 23 (+21% YoY); guides 18-20% lead growth, EBITDA/kg Rs 19-20 stable.  
- Aluminium weak (-33% revenue YoY, -43% volumes); plastics revenue -39% YoY but EBITDA/kg improves to Rs 10.

**Growth Outlook**  
- Q4FY26 volume growth guided at 8-9%; long-term revenue/APAT CAGRs ~18%/~26% with RoCE ~18%.  
- Vision 2029: 25%+ volume CAGR, 35%+ profitability CAGR FY25-FY29; 50% revenue from value-added products by FY29.  
- Tailwinds from rising scrap availability, BMWR/EPR/RCM policies, EV/clean energy demand favoring Mundra/new lines.

**Valuation & View**  
- Trades at 33x/27x FY27E/FY28E EPS; FY28E EPS Rs 71.3, RoCE ~18%, RoNW 17-18%.  
- Mirae Asset Sharekhan: Buy, TP Rs 2,000 (28x FY28E EPS, ~25% upside).

**Key Risks**  
- Regulatory issues around scrap procurement and policy changes impacting sourcing.  
- Global aluminium price volatility tightening scrap availability/processing volumes.

**Key Takeaway**: Gravita's copper/Li-ion push atop lead strength sets up 25%+ volume CAGR to FY29, but execution amid aluminium volatility and regulatory risks will determine re-rating potential.

South Korea has been on a tear. India continues to lag global markets.

Source: Neil Borate

**Why Businesses Fail: 2 Simple Financial Rules**

**Rule 1: ROCE > Cost of Capital**  
Money costs 12-15% (equity/debt).  
**ROCE 10% vs CoC 14% = 4% value destruction annually.**  
Sustainable wealth only when ROCE consistently exceeds CoC.  

**Rule 2: Cash In Before Cash Out**  
Profits ≠ cash.  
**Short-term debt for long-term assets creates repayment mismatches.**  
OCF must cover debt servicing needs for stability.  

**Simple Test**  
- ROCE > CoC → **Value creation**  
- OCF ≥ Debt needs → **Financial stability**  

**Key Takeaway:**  
90% failures trace to these gaps. Master both rules = survive cycles, compound wealth.
